steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content:
Since lines $m$ and $n$ are parallel and line $\ell$ is a transversal cutting through both, the angles $x^{\circ}$ and $26^{\circ}$ are on the same side of the transversal and between the parallel lines.

These are co-interior angles (also called consecutive interior angles or same-side interior angles).

steps.title: Apply the co-interior angle theorem and solve
steps.content:
When a transversal crosses two parallel lines, co-interior angles are supplementary—they sum to $180^{\circ}$:
$$x + 26 = 180$$
$$x = 154$$
That is the correct answer.
